name: code-review-checklist
description: Comprehensive checklist for conducting thorough code reviews covering functionality, security, performance, and maintainability
capabilities: Provides comprehensive checklists covering functionality, security, performance, and code quality
Includes specific examples of common vulnerabilities and how to identify them during reviews
Offers review comment templates for requesting changes, asking questions, and praising good code
Covers pre-review preparation, git workflow, and documentation requirements
Teaches reviewers how to identify SQL injection, XSS, and authentication issues
Includes best practices for providing constructive feedback during code reviews
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a benign code review checklist educational resource. All static analyzer findings are FALSE POSITIVES triggered by example code snippets in documentation. The skill teaches security best practices by showing examples of vulnerable code patterns (SQL injection, hardcoded secrets) to help developers identify and avoid them during reviews.
valueStatement: This skill provides systematic checklists for conducting thorough code reviews, helping reviewers catch bugs, identify security issues, and maintain code quality standards across any codebase.
userTitle: Master Code Reviews with Comprehensive Checklists
limitations: Does not execute or test code - provides guidance only
Does not integrate with CI/CD systems or automate review workflows
Does not provide automatic vulnerability scanning or security testing
Checklists are generic and may need customization for specific project requirements

useCases.title: Pull Request Reviews
useCases.description: Use the checklists to systematically review pull requests and ensure nothing is missed before approving changes.

useCases.title: Security Audits
useCases.description: Apply the security-focused checklist sections to identify potential vulnerabilities in code before deployment.

useCases.title: Team Onboarding
useCases.description: Train new team members on code review standards and best practices using the comprehensive checklist framework.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the code review checklist to review these changes: [paste diff or describe changes]. Focus on functionality, security, and code quality.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick review of a small PR or code changes

promptTemplates.title: Security-Focused Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a security review of this code using the security checklist. Look for SQL injection, XSS, authentication issues, hardcoded secrets, and input validation: [paste code]
promptTemplates.scenario: Dedicated security review of sensitive code changes

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Conduct a thorough code review using all checklist sections: functionality, security, performance, code quality, tests, and documentation. Review these changes: [paste diff or describe changes]
promptTemplates.scenario: Thorough review of major changes or critical code

promptTemplates.title: Review Feedback
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write constructive review comments for the following issues found during code review: [list issues]. Use the review comment templates from the checklist.

bestPractices: Review small, focused changes rather than large PRs to ensure thoroughness
Always check tests first - verify they pass and adequately cover new code
Use automated tools like linters and security scanners in addition to manual review
Focus on important issues like security and logic errors rather than minor style issues
antiPatterns: Do not approve code without actually reading and understanding it
Do not skip security checks - security vulnerabilities are critical
Do not provide vague feedback without specific examples or suggestions
Do not rubber stamp reviews - every review should add value

faq.question: What is a code review checklist?
faq.answer: A code review checklist is a systematic list of items to check during code reviews, covering functionality, security, performance, code quality, and other important aspects to ensure thorough and consistent reviews.

faq.question: Why should I use a code review checklist?
faq.answer: Checklists help ensure nothing is missed during reviews, provide consistency across different reviewers, and help train new developers on what to look for in code reviews.

faq.question: What security issues should I look for?
faq.answer: Key security issues include SQL injection, XSS vulnerabilities, improper authentication/authorization, hardcoded secrets, missing input validation, and insecure dependency usage.

faq.question: How do I provide constructive feedback?
faq.answer: Be specific about issues, show examples of problematic code, suggest improvements, explain why changes are needed, and acknowledge good code practices you see.

faq.question: Can I customize the checklist for my team?
faq.answer: Yes, the checklist is a template that should be customized to fit your team's specific needs, coding standards, and project requirements.

faq.question: What should I do if I find a security vulnerability?
faq.answer: Mark the review as requiring changes, clearly explain the security issue and its potential impact, provide a secure alternative, and consider escalating to your security team for critical issues.
